assertions.text: CONTRACTOR is either placed in a separate @TableTest (not mixed with the level×department grid) OR the response explicitly notes the structural asymmetry and explains the design choice
assertions.evidence: The CONTRACTOR rule is a natural fit for a value set since it holds regardless of department ... **Value set on CONTRACTOR**: `{SALES, ENGINEERING}` generates two rows, both asserting `0.00` — directly expressing "regardless of department" without listing every combination manually.

assertions.text: If CONTRACTOR appears in the same table as SENIOR/JUNIOR rows, the department value is not a dummy or placeholder (e.g. 'ANY', 'N/A') just to fill the grid
The four level×department combinations (SENIOR+SALES, SENIOR+ENGINEERING, JUNIOR+SALES, JUNIOR+ENGINEERING) are all present in the table(s)
assertions.evidence:
Senior in Sales        | SENIOR     | SALES                | 0.15
    Senior in Engineering  | SENIOR     | ENGINEERING          | 0.12
    Junior in Sales        | JUNIOR     | SALES                | 0.08
    Junior in Engineering  | JUNIOR     | ENGINEERING          | 0.05

assertions.text: Test method body contains no if or switch statements
